item.title: Gerry Goffin
item.description: Era: 1960s–1990s (primary period: 1960–1967) Genres: Girl group, Pop soul, Rock & roll, soul Key collaborator: Carole King (co-writer and wife, 1959–1968) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Gerry Goffin was the lyricist of the most prolific and versatile songwriting team in the Brill Building — the par...

item.title: The Rolling Stones
item.description: Genres: British blues, blues rock, rock & roll, roots rock Scene: London (Richmond, then Soho) Era: 1962–present (primary period: 1968–1972) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Keith Richards plays behind the beat.

item.title: Swamp blues
item.description: Period1954–1970LocationBaton Rouge, recorded in Crowley, LouisianaInfluencesElectric bluesZydecoCajun musicLinksRYM The laziest beat in the blues: a shuffle that sounds half-asleep, drums brushed down to a whisper, tremolo guitar hanging in the humidity, and a singer who’d rather insinuate than sho...

item.title: Piano blues
item.description: Periodc. 1900–presentLocationThe barrelhouse circuit, then Chicago, St.

item.title: Gold Star Studios
item.description: Location: 6252 Santa Monica Boulevard, Hollywood, California Years of peak activity: 1950s–1984 (demolished 1984) Key engineers: Larry Levine, Stan Ross, Dave Gold Gold Star’s echo chambers, a pair of trapezoidal concrete rooms built directly behind Studio A, were the studio’s secret instrument: fee...
